content:
Practical guidance for “Community teaching board” within “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”. It supports clear responsibilities, verified information, visible records, fair priorities, stop conditions, and a workable handover, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Community teaching board” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Stop condition: Do not expose private identity, health, location, or contact details beyond genuine need. Apply this boundary to “Community teaching board” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Reduced fallback: If “Community teaching board” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a smaller documented task with one owner, one deadline, and one independent check.

Separate verified facts from assumptions, requests, and unconfirmed reports. Recheck this point before closing “Community teaching board” for “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Stop and reassess “Community teaching board” when an unverified report changes decisions without a source, time, or confirmation. Apply this boundary to “Community teaching board” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.
